stimulus: Cherokee weaver Shan Goshorn’s basket Educational Genocide is woven from strips of paper printed with archival photographs of nineteenth-century boarding schools; each strip carries a different image of a different student. The arrangement of the strips, according to ______blank embodies her conviction that “every face deserves to be remembered, name by name.”
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The phrase "according to Goshorn" is a parenthetical aside interrupting the sentence and must be enclosed in matched punctuation. Since a comma already precedes "according," the closing mark must also be a comma.

Choice A is incorrect because a dash cannot pair with an opening comma to enclose a parenthetical aside. Choice C is incorrect because no punctuation fails to close the aside opened by the comma. Choice D is incorrect because "The arrangement of the strips, according to Goshorn" is not an independent clause and cannot precede a colon.
